name: Sammy Davis Jr.
abstract: Samuel George Davis Jr. (December 8, 1925 – May 16, 1990) was an American singer, actor, comedian and dancer.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: Two months after I joined the band we were sitting on the same stage at Harrah’s in Lake Tahoe with Sammy Davis. And every night I got to play three songs with Sammy
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I went out for four years, 1972 to ’76 with Sammy... I couldn’t wait to get on the stand at night... half of the show was just him and piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: It was a Sammy Davis movie. It went nowhere. But the music was good. Benny did the score for a movie, and Mel Torme was in the movie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Byron Stripling
about_quotes.evidence: like Sammy Davis, who I didn’t get to see, but if these people were on T.V.
about_quotes.doc_title: Byron Stripling interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 25, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Pallo
about_quotes.evidence: And playing with Sammy Davis, oh I have a good story for you. I only did one week with Sammy Davis and the music would be piled up this high, and we would rehearse the — George Rhodes I think was the orchestra that I played with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Pallo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Louie Bellson had a lot to do with getting me to be Sammy Davis Jr.’s drummer, and I was with Sammy for three years off and on at that point.
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995
